creator: zarychtam@plan-b.pwste.edu.pl
text:
This port builds fine without the patch[1]. Removing this patch doesn't fix the described issue though. The keyring pam module still expects the old directory[2] to create the trusted file.

Maybe the FreeBSD Foundation can sponsor resolving this issue by making the pam_xdg path more configurable. It's strictly bound with the recently announced expansion into the laptop user base. 

1. https://cgit.freebsd.org/ports/tree/security/gnome-keyring/files/patch-pam_gkr-pam-client.c

2. Oct 16 17:20:42 fomalhaut gnome-keyring-daemon[4885]: couldn't access control socket: /var/run/user/1001/keyring/control: No such file or directory
